title: Known Conflicts for Generic Procurement Contract MVP
description: Lightweight, source-grounded conflicts for testing multi-source fusion, naming normalization, rule comparison, field ambiguity and missing evidence detection. This is not a scoring script.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Business documents define Supplier as the external provider.
DDL uses pc_supplier and supplier_* column names.
Code method parameter uses vendor for a Supplier object.
Glossary maps Supplier to Vendor as another term.

conflicts.description: The same external provider concept appears as Supplier in DDL/documents and as Vendor in parts of the code. This is a naming inconsistency, not a separate business concept.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Normalize Vendor to canonical Supplier. Preserve Vendor as an alias and do not create a separate Vendor concept.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit if the AI merges Supplier and Vendor under one concept with alias/mapping. Penalize duplicate Supplier and Vendor concepts or treating Vendor as an unrelated entity.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Document uses Purchase Request as the business object name.
Code uses PR abbreviation in service method names.
CSV request numbers use PR-2026-* format.
Glossary states PR means Purchase Request.

conflicts.description: Purchase Request appears as full business term, code abbreviation PR, and PR-prefixed identifiers in sample data.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Map PR and purchase requisition to PurchaseRequest. Do not confuse PR with PurchaseOrder/PO.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for recognizing PR as PurchaseRequest. Penalize merging PR with PO or treating PR as a separate concept.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: DDL uses goods_receipt and receipt_* fields.
Document says warehouse users often call Goods Receipt a GRN.
CSV receipt numbers use GRN-2026-* identifiers.
Glossary states GRN means Goods Receipt Note.

conflicts.description: Goods Receipt is named as goods_receipt in DDL, GRN in warehouse/business terminology, and GRN-* in CSV identifiers.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Normalize GRN and Goods Receipt Note to GoodsReceipt. Preserve GRN as alias; do not create a separate GRN concept.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for alias normalization. Penalize duplicate GoodsReceipt and GRN concepts.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: DDL uses payment_request naming.
Code method uses Payment Application terminology.
Document says finance users may call Payment Request a Payment Application.
Glossary maps Payment Request to Payment Application.

conflicts.description: The same finance object appears as Payment Request in DDL and gold ontology, and as Payment Application in documents/code.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Map Payment Application to canonical Payment/PaymentRequest. Do not create a separate PaymentApplication concept.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for alias normalization and relation to Invoice. Penalize duplicate PaymentRequest and PaymentApplication concepts.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Document says greater than or equal to 50000 CNY requires finance manager approval.
Business rules say requests greater than or equal to 50000 CNY require finance manager approval.
DDL comment says PR amount >= 50000 requires finance manager approval.
Code checks estimatedAmount.compareTo(50000) > 0, routing only amounts greater than 50000 to finance manager.

conflicts.description: Documents and DDL use a >= 50000 threshold for finance approval, while code uses > 50000. The boundary case exactly 50000 differs.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Identify this as a boundary mismatch. Prefer documenting the conflict rather than silently choosing one rule. A robust output should mention that exactly 50000 CNY is ambiguous across sources.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award strong credit if the AI detects the >= vs > mismatch and records a conflict/uncertainty. Partial credit if it extracts high-value approval but misses the boundary issue. Penalize confidently stating one boundary without provenance.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Document says allowed invoice variance is 2% of Purchase Order amount unless finance grants an exception.
Business rules mention variance tolerance but do not override the 2% policy text.
Code sets CODE_MATCH_TOLERANCE_RATE to 0.015, meaning 1.5% tolerance.

conflicts.description: Invoice matching tolerance is 2% in policy documentation but 1.5% in source code.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Record this as a code-versus-document rule mismatch. Do not average the values or silently choose one without source attribution.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award strong credit for identifying the 2% vs 1.5% mismatch. Partial credit if both values are extracted separately. Penalize hallucinated tolerances or ignoring the conflict while claiming high confidence.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Document says contract is active only after legal approval and signature by both parties.
DDL stores legal_approved_at and activated_at but no explicit signed_by_supplier, signed_by_company or signed_date field.
Code activation requires legalApprovedAt and contract term dates, but no explicit signature check.

conflicts.description: Business document requires both-party signature before contract activation, but DDL and code do not contain explicit signature fields or signature validation.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Keep Contract activation rule but mark signature evidence as missing/uncertain. Do not invent signature fields in the schema or ontology.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for detecting missing structural/code evidence for signature requirement. Penalize adding unsupported signed_by or signature_status attributes as if they existed in input data.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: DDL contains item_description, category_code, quantity, UOM and price but no material master table.
CSV line items contain laptop, scanner, service and safety item descriptions.
Business object list mentions Purchase Request Line but not a standalone Material master object.

conflicts.description: Material semantics exist in request line descriptions and category codes, but there is no standalone material table or material lifecycle evidence.
conflicts.expected_resolution: If Material is extracted, it should be represented as a lower-confidence semantic concept derived from request line fields, not as a fully supported master-data object with invented IDs or lifecycle.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for cautious Material extraction with evidence from line fields. Penalize hallucinating a material master table, material_id, stock status, or unsupported lifecycle.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: DDL table is pc_user with user_id, user_name and role_code.
Document describes business roles such as Requester, Department Manager and Finance Manager.
CSV stores employees/users with role_code values.
Business overview lists Department and User as organizational actors.

conflicts.description: The same row in pc_user represents an internal employee/user, while role_code represents the user role. User/Employee and Role should be related but not merged.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Model Employee/User as actor concept and role_code/roles as role or permission concepts. Do not create separate concepts for each person name or merge Employee with Role.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for separating Employee/User from Role and Permission. Penalize treating role_code values as employees, or treating named sample users as ontology concepts.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Document defines receipt status flow PENDING -> PARTIAL -> COMPLETE and PENDING -> REJECTED.
DDL has receipt_status values PENDING, PARTIAL, COMPLETE, REJECTED.
DDL has purchase order statuses RELEASED, PARTIALLY_RECEIVED, CLOSED, CANCELLED.
CSV shows PO status CLOSED while receipt status uses COMPLETE in goods receipt rows.

conflicts.description: Receipt completion uses COMPLETE, while purchase order completion uses CLOSED. These statuses are related but belong to different lifecycle owners.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Keep COMPLETE as GoodsReceipt state and CLOSED as PurchaseOrder state. Do not merge receipt_status and po_status state sets into one generic closed/completed state machine.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for assigning statuses to the correct owner concept. Penalize global status merging that loses whether COMPLETE belongs to receipt and CLOSED belongs to PO.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: Document says held payment requires a hold reason in operational notes, although the MVP table stores only status.
DDL has payment_status but no hold_reason or operational_notes column.
CSV includes HELD payments but no hold reason column.

conflicts.description: Business policy mentions hold reason for held payments, but DDL and CSV do not store a hold reason field.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Record hold reason as a documented rule with missing structural evidence. Do not add hold_reason as a confirmed Payment attribute unless explicitly marked uncertain or missing.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for noting missing field evidence. Penalize confidently adding hold_reason as a standard schema attribute without uncertainty.

involved_sources.evidence_summary: DDL uses target_type and target_id instead of explicit foreign keys to purchase request, contract or payment request.
CSV target_type values include PURCHASE_REQUEST, CONTRACT and PAYMENT_REQUEST.
Role matrix describes approvals for normal PR, high-value PR, legal review and payment application.

conflicts.description: Approval record uses polymorphic target_type/target_id rather than explicit foreign keys, so relations to PurchaseRequest, Contract and Payment must be inferred from target_type values.
conflicts.expected_resolution: Model ApprovalTask as a workflow decision that can target multiple business objects. Mark relations inferred from target_type as lower-confidence than explicit FK relations.
conflicts.scoring_guidance: Award credit for modeling polymorphic approval target carefully. Penalize assuming target_id always references PurchaseRequest or ignoring contract/payment approval records.

usage_notes: These conflicts are intentionally lightweight and explainable.
They should be used to evaluate whether an AI output preserves uncertainty and source provenance instead of forcing a single unsupported answer.
This file provides scoring guidance text, but it is not an executable scoring script.
